seoTitle: How to Insert a Calendar in a Confluence Page
metaDescription: Embed a Team Calendar in Confluence, link Jira deadlines, and sync external Outlook or Google calendars for a live project schedule.
intro: Tracking project deadlines in scattered bullet lists makes it easy to lose sight of what's coming up. This guide covers embedding a live Team Calendar directly inside a Confluence page, linking it to Jira deadlines, and syncing external calendars like Outlook or Google Calendar.

steps.heading: Open your target page
steps.body: Open your documentation page, enter edit mode, and click your insertion point.

steps.heading: Deploy the calendar macro
steps.body: Type the /team calendar slash command to deploy the interactive schedule frame.

steps.heading: Add or create a calendar
steps.body: Search and add an existing space calendar, or click Create New Team Calendar to start fresh.

steps.heading: Set your preferred view
steps.body: Choose your display view — month, week, or list — from the right-side options bar.

steps.heading: Sync external calendars
steps.body: Click Add Calendar to paste a secure Google or Outlook ICS sync token and bring external events into the page.

steps.heading: Publish the schedule
steps.body: Click the blue Publish button to finalize and present the interactive schedule panel to your team.

faq.question: Can I link my Confluence calendar to Jira deadlines?
faq.answer: Yes, you can cross-link Jira software filter dates so task changes reflect across your documentation dashboards automatically.

faq.question: Can I sync a Google Calendar into a Confluence page?
faq.answer: Yes, the Add Calendar option accepts an ICS sync token from Google Calendar or Outlook to pull in external events.

faq.question: Can I control who edits the team calendar?
faq.answer: Yes, you can manage permission states so only authorized administrators can adjust launch dates on the calendar.

faq.question: What view options does the Confluence team calendar support?
faq.answer: You can display it in month, week, or list view depending on how much detail you want visible at once.
